title: Staging API Gateway 502 — jsdom@27 ESM crash on Node 20
context: Deploy Staging workflow (run 24036780682) passed all build steps but API Gateway returned 502 for the full 420s health-check timeout. MCP, web-dashboard, and nginx were all healthy. The container crash-loops because Node 20 cannot require() an ESM-only module introduced by jsdom@27's dependency chain.

chains.symptom: API Gateway returns 502 on staging for 420 seconds, then health check fails the deploy
whys: Why 1: The API Gateway container crash-loops on startup with ERR_REQUIRE_ASYNC_MODULE
Why 2: cssstyle@5.x depends on @asamuzakjp/css-color which uses ESM top-level await — Node 20 cannot require() it
Why 3: jsdom@27 depends on cssstyle@5.x (breaking change from cssstyle@4.x which was CJS-compatible)
Why 4: isomorphic-dompurify@2.36.0 (resolved fresh in runner stage) requires jsdom@^27.0.0 as a direct dependency

Why 5 (ROOT): The Dockerfile runner stage runs pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile against a generated package.json, bypassing the workspace lockfile. pnpm.overrides placed in apps/api-gateway/package.json was silently ignored because pnpm only reads overrides from the workspace root.
chains.root_cause: pnpm.overrides in a workspace sub-package is silently ignored. The runner stage Docker install resolves dependencies fresh (no lockfile), so isomorphic-dompurify floated to 2.36.0 pulling jsdom@27 → cssstyle@5 → ESM-only code incompatible with Node 20.
chains.fix: Move jsdom override to root package.json pnpm.overrides: "jsdom": "^26.1.0". Verified via pnpm why jsdom — all resolutions now 26.1.0, cssstyle resolves to 4.6.0 (CJS).
chains.verification: pnpm why jsdom shows 26.1.0 across entire workspace. cssstyle@4.6.0 confirmed. Lockfile updated. Deploy triggered (run 24038043896).

chains.symptom: First fix attempt (commit 4dc9bda) did not resolve the 502
whys: Why 1: The override was placed in apps/api-gateway/package.json
Why 2: pnpm workspace architecture only reads overrides from the root package.json
Why 3: pnpm emits a WARN but does not error — easy to miss in CI logs
Why 4: The builder stage resolved jsdom@26.1.0 from the lockfile (masking the problem), but the runner stage resolved fresh without lockfile
Why 5 (ROOT): Two-stage Docker install creates a divergence: builder uses --frozen-lockfile (safe), runner uses --no-frozen-lockfile against a generated package.json (unsafe, floats versions)
chains.root_cause: The runner stage Dockerfile pattern (--no-frozen-lockfile with generated package.json) is inherently fragile — it can float transitive dependencies to breaking versions even when the builder stage is pinned via lockfile.
chains.fix: Immediate: root-level pnpm override pins jsdom. Future: investigate making the runner stage use a pruned lockfile instead of --no-frozen-lockfile to prevent similar drift.

prevention.immediate: Root-level pnpm override pins jsdom to ^26.1.0 across the entire workspace
prevention.short_term: Add CI check that warns when runner stage resolves a different major version than builder stage
prevention.long_term: Refactor Dockerfile runner stage to use a pruned lockfile (pnpm deploy or pnpm install --lockfile-only) instead of --no-frozen-lockfile, eliminating version drift entirely
